steps.title: Use the zero-product shortcut
steps.content: The product $(5x+4)(2x-5)$ equals $0$, so at least one factor must equal $0$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $-\frac{5}{2}$, $-\frac{5}{2}$, has the wrong sign.
  • Choice B — $-\frac{5}{4}$, $-\frac{5}{4}$, does not make either factor $0$.
  • Choice C — $-\frac{4}{5}$, $-\frac{4}{5}$, makes $5x+4=0$. Correct.
  • Choice D — $-\frac{2}{5}$, $-\frac{2}{5}$, does not make either factor $0$.
That matches Choice C — $-\frac{4}{5}$.
